entry.title: Re: SAP COLLECTIONS MANAGEMENT INITIAL DATA TRANSFER FROM...
entry.content: Sending of FI-AR-Relevant Data to Collections Management is done automatically by a background job(FDM_COLL_SEND_ITEMS) running daily in update mode and weekly run in rebuild mode. This is a standard system behaviour. There will be an interim period where these credit memos will still show up in the App Process Receivables. After the next run of the rebuild invoice memory, the entries should be gone.

entry.title: Value Unleashed: How to activate applications in SAP Signavio Lab Space
entry.content:
Exploring the SAP Signavio Lab Space is a valuable opportunity to experience the future of process management today. With a signed Test and Evaluation Agreement (TEA) in place, you can activate prototype applications and gain early access to innovative tools before they’re officially released. This allows you to experiment, provide meaningful feedback, and directly influence SAP’s product development. It’s a simple and cost-free way to try out cutting-edge features like “Text to Process”, helping your organization stay agile, forward-thinking, and competitive.
Give it a try it’s easier than you think and delivers real strategic value!

Step 1) Copy Your Workspace ID
You’ll need this to request access to Lab Space applications.

Step 2) Open the SAP Signavio Lab Space
Navigate to the Lab Space environment from your Signavio interface.

Step 3) Choose an Application to Test
Select one of the available experimental applications—for example, "Text to Process".

Step 4) Submit Your Request
Follow the on-screen instructions. If your email client does not open automatically, manually send an email to the provided contact. Be sure to include:

  • Your Workspace ID

  • A short note describing which application you’d like to test and why

Step 5) Send the Email
Make sure all information is accurate before submitting your request.

Step 6) Wait for Access & Start Exploring
After a review period, access will be granted and you can begin using the application.

Enjoy exploring the Lab Space – and don't forget to share your feedback!
You can find the feedback button conveniently located in the bottom-right corner of your Lab Space interface.

entry.title: Work Experience Portlet & RBP Restrictions Limitations in SAP SF Spotlight
entry.content:
In Work Experience card, a user can view field values from Internal Job History for which the user does not have permission to. This is expected behaviour currently, field level permissions are not respected in the Work Experience card.

A key limitation to be aware of when using SAP Spotlight with SuccessFactors Work Experience (Internal Job History) data is the lack of enforcement of Role-Based Permission (RBP) restrictions based on assignment groups.

What this means in practice:

  1. Users from one region or assignment group may be able to view Work Experience data belonging to other regions, potentially exposing sensitive employee information.
  2. This poses a GDPR compliance risk when data visibility crosses geographic or organizational boundaries unintentionally.
  3. The Work Experience portlet is also not supported on the Full latest People Profile UI from 2H 2025, limiting functionality and experience.

Current workaround:

As of now we have 2 workarounds for the Standard Work Experience portlet:

1. To restrict the visibility of the Work Experience section in Spotlight is by adjusting this RBP permission: Employee Central Effective Dated Entities > Job Information Actions (View Current)

However, this workaround hides users' job information entirely, not just the Work Experience section.

2. Go to Admin Center > Configure Latest People Profile > Choose the Spotlight option > Disable Visibility of Spotlight card.

Recommended Long-Term Solution

For customers facing this issue with the standard Internal Job History portlet, a viable approach is to:

  • Create a custom MDF object
  • Flow data from Job Information to this custom MDF object
  • Display the custom MDF object on the Latest People Profile instead of the standard Work Experience card

This approach respects RBP restrictions and aligns with the new People Profile UI.

We strongly recommend customers submit an enhancement request to SAP for introducing RBP support in the Work Experience card in Spotlight.

If you want these RBP issue to be fix for the future enhancements, SAP team will consider all enhancement requests raised in the community and will consider this request as an option for a future release.

Please see the attached KBA 2090228 - How to Submit Ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products on how to do this.

entry.title: SAP Fiori tools 2506 update – Open source, faster app creation, adaptation boost, AI enhancements
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the 2506 release of SAP Fiori tools, bringing enhancements that make full-stack development with SAP Fiori elements and RAP even more seamless—from initial app creation to UI adaptation and low-code configuration. Whether you're just starting your project or looking to fine-tune a production app, this update continues our focus on productivity, extensibility, and user experience.

Let’s take a closer look at what’s new in this release.

SAP Fiori generator: Now fully open source

After a long journey of gradual transition, the SAP Fiori Application Generator is now fully open source. This means all the core logic that powers the generator has been moved to a public repository, giving you full transparency and the opportunity to contribute directly.

If you’re curious about how it works under the hood or want to propose improvements, now is the perfect time to get involved. Learn more in this dedicated blog post.

💡Tip: If you rely on custom generators or extensions, this is a great opportunity to align your tools more closely with the open-source foundation.

Accelerating RAP development with Quick Fiori App

Deploy a ready to use SAP Fiori elements app with ADT, and import it into your dev environment with SAP Fiori tools if tweaks are needed.

To simplify the initial stages of app development, you can now quickly generate a working SAP Fiori elements application based on a RAP business object—directly from ADT. With just a few clicks, a basic app is deployed using the service's annotations, giving you a functional starting point in record time. And when you’re ready to go further, you can import the app into SAP Business Application Studio or Visual Studio Code using SAP Fiori tools, continue customizing, and redeploy as usual.

This feature is available starting with SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2505 and SAP Fiori tools version 1.17.6.

Enhancing adaptation projects with local annotations and more

Use quick actions to open the right adaptation dialog for your task.

UI adaptation projects continue to grow more powerful. This release introduces support for changes that typically involve modifications to the manifest.json—such as creating new pages—now available directly via quick actions in the adaptation editor.

We also added support for creating local annotation files within adaptation projects. Whether you're defining new views or tweaking behavior, you now benefit from the same intelligent editing experience provided by the Language Server Protocol (LSP), including auto-completion, code snippets, and quick fixes—making it easier than ever to stay within the Fiori elements design guidelines.

The outline tree and property panel have also received a usability upgrade, with more quick actions and context menu options—particularly for working within tables.

Smarter tooling and continuous enhancements with AI

The SAP Fiori generator now defaults to using virtual endpoints in newly created projects, simplifying preview configuration and aligning better with modern dev environments.

The AI-powered Project Accelerator continues to evolve as well. It now supports Word documents containing both text and images, giving you even more flexibility when starting a project from design inputs. You can also decide exactly where the generated project should be placed in your workspace, putting you in full control of your file structure.

In the Application Modeler, our low-code editor for SAP Fiori elements, we’ve expanded the configuration options for micro charts. You can now define sort criteria for chart columns and use additional chart types, including “Column,” “Comparison,” and “Harvey.” These changes make it easier to present complex data visually with minimal effort.

Finally, when deploying to Cloud Foundry, you now have the option to select “Application Frontend” deployment within SAP Fiori tools, streamlining the deployment flow even further.

Looking ahead

The future of SAP Fiori tools continues to be shaped by your feedback and evolving development needs. We're working on:

  • Bringing new capabilities to the Project Accelerator, including the ability to extract app information from PDFs, and enhanced Application Modeler’s editing capabilities.
  • Deeper alignment between RAP and SAP Fiori development to further streamline the full-stack development workflow.
  • Better in-tool troubleshooting during development, helping you catch issues earlier.
  • Extended support for SAP Fiori elements features in the Application Modeler for a more complete low-code experience.

As always, we welcome your input and questions in the SAP Fiori elements and tools Community.

We hope these updates help you build great apps faster and more efficiently. Let us know what you think!

If you want to learn about updates to our SAP Fiori tools and technologies, please sign up for the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

entry.title: Configure Governance Rules in SAP Build
entry.content:
Disclaimer: The insights shared in this blog are based on my personal observations and learning. They may not apply universally, and I encourage readers to conduct their own research and make an informed decision before using this content for productive use.
 
Introduction
As organizations increasingly adopt low-code/no-code platforms like SAP Build Process Automation, the ability for developers to independently create and deploy automation projects across systems has brought both speed and efficiency to operations. With minimal technical barriers, business users and developers alike can prototype and launch automations rapidly—streamlining approvals, triggering backend processes, and replacing manual tasks.
However, this empowerment introduces a new set of challenges, particularly in managing what happens after a project is built. In traditional software delivery, deployments to critical systems—like production—follow tightly controlled paths with approvals, validations, and audits. But in environments with rapid automation, these controls are often missing or too lightweight.
 
Business Challenge:
In SAP Build Process Automation, users with the Developer role have the ability to:
• Create multiple automation projects.
• Transport projects between environments, including into production.
While this flexibility is essential for innovation, it raises a serious governance concern: Who is monitoring these transports? Who ensures that only validated, reviewed projects reach production? Without safeguards, even an accidental deployment can cause business process disruptions, data inconsistencies, or security loopholes.
Earlier, there was no built-in mechanism to regulate or oversee such activities. As a result, organizations relied heavily on manual communication, post-facto monitoring, or restrictive access controls—which either slowed down innovation or left gaps in accountability.
 
The Turning Point: Governance Rules
To address this gap, Governance Rules were introduced. This feature allows administrators to define structured approval processes and enforce checks before a project is transported between systems—especially into sensitive environments like production.
With Governance Rules, you can:
• Create mandatory approval workflows before project transport.
• Specify who can approve transports based on project type, environment, or organizational policy.
• Track and audit all transport requests, decisions, and outcomes.
• Enable role-based control without completely blocking development agility.
This creates a safety net that ensures only reviewed and authorized changes make it into business-critical systems.
 
Business Impact:
The introduction of Governance Rules has shifted the paradigm from reactive issue management to proactive risk prevention. Organizations benefit from:  
• Improved compliance with internal policies and external regulations.
• Reduced risk of unintentional disruptions in production.
• Clear audit trails for all project movements and approvals.
• Increased confidence in scaling automation across departments.
Moreover, it encourages a culture of shared responsibility—developers retain the ability to build freely, while governance teams ensure stability and alignment with enterprise standards.
 
Prerequisites
You’ve created a service instance and service key for SAP Build Process Automation. Note down the service key credentials for configuring the destination.
You've the Process Automation Admin role, which allows you to perform these steps.
You've set up the destination and added the following properties in your SAP BTP subaccount.

Destination Configuration
FieldValue
NameAs per your choice. For example, sap_process_automation_service_user_token_exchange.
TypeHTTP
URL<”endpoints”.”api”>
Proxy TypeInternet
AuthenticationOAuth2UserTokenExchange
Client ID<"uaa"."clientid”>
Client Secret<"uaa"."clientsecret”>
Token Service URL<"uaa"."url">/oauth/token
Additional Properties 
FieldValue
sap.processautomation.enabledTRUE
 
 
Ensure you've created the environment and allow-list the destination in the Governance environment by navigating to Control Tower > Destinations > Add. Search and select the destination, click Next, select 'Specific Environment', select 'Governance', and then click
'Add Destination'.
 
 
 
 
 
Now, click on the Governance Rule tile under Control Tower.
 
 
Now, you can choose the scenario for which you want to activate the Governance Rule. You have the option to set it up for both Create Project and Transport Project. By default, the setting is Ungoverned. To switch to Governed, you’ll need to create the project by clicking the Create button.
 

 

 

A pre-configured project will be created automatically. You can customize it by adding additional validations or modifying parameters such as the approver or you can directly deploy it to the Governance Environment.
 
 
 
While deploying, select the destination(sap_process_automation_service_user_token_exchange) that was created and added under your Build Control Tower.

 

 

 
Now, if you go back and check under Governance Rules, you'll see that the Governed option is enabled. Select it and click Confirm.
 

 

 
The setup for Transport Project Governance is now complete. From now on, whenever someone attempts to transport a project from the Development system to the Production system, the defined governance rules will be enforced.
 

 

 

 
After clicking on Transport, a questionnaire form will appear, which the submitter needs to fill out and submit for review.
 
 

 

 

 

 

Once the request is submitted, admins can view all requests along with their current statuses. Only the designated approver has the authority to approve or reject the request.
 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion:
Governance Rules are not just a feature—they represent a strategic enabler for controlled automation growth. By embedding checks, balances, and transparency into the deployment lifecycle, businesses can confidently expand their automation footprint without compromising on control or compliance. In fast-paced environments where change is constant, Governance Rules serve as a foundation for sustainable, secure, and scalable innovation.

entry.title: Task Center ECC Connector – Bridging the Gap During Your S/4HANA Transition
entry.content:
Are ECC-based workflows causing bottlenecks in your S/4HANA migration journey? You're not without a solution.

Introducing the ECC Connector for Task Center, delivered in partnership with MHP, a valuable enabler for customers navigating hybrid SAP landscapes. Whether you're mid-migration or working in a dual-system environment, this connector helps integrate your classic ECC tasks directly into the modern SAP Task Center experience on BTP.

What Is the ECC Connector?

The ECC Connector is a purpose-built solution designed to seamlessly pull tasks from ECC systems into SAP Task Center—your central inbox for all workflow items across SAP and third-party systems.

Built on SAP BTP CAP architecture and deployed directly into your SAP and BTP infrastructure, the connector supports:

  • Pulling ECC-based approval tasks

  • Operating through My Inbox actions (including bulk operations)

  • Detailed task data including attachments and notifications

  • Backend extensibility for customer-specific enhancements

What’s Delivered as an ECC connector?

As part of the implementation, MHP provides:

  • BTP CAP API Interface deployed on your SAP BTP instance

  • Backend Interface in the MHP namespace for ECC/SAP on-premise systems

  • Full implementation support

  • Tailored adjustments to your business requirements

Scope & Eligibility

Your organization qualifies if:

  • You have a BTP subaccount with Task Center and Backend Connectivity enabled (via Cloud Connector & Principal Propagation)

  • You're running My Inbox on ECC for existing approval processes

  • Your SAP system meets minimum version: SAP_BASIS, SAP_ABA, SAP_GWFND ≥ 750

Benefits and Roadmap

Current Capabilities Include:

  • Pulling tasks from ECC backend

  • Support for My Inbox actions (approve/reject)

  • Attachments, details, and multi-backend support

  • Easy display integration for My Inbox detail views

What's Next:

  • Adjustments based on early customer feedback

  • Enhanced support for multiple backend systems, clients, and 3rd party SaaS

  • Full SaaS readiness with 24/7 support, SAP Store listing, and standard rollout models

Why ECC Connector is crutial during S/4HANA Migration?

During your migration to S/4HANA, it's not uncommon to maintain hybrid landscapes where legacy ECC processes coexist with modern SAP BTP components. Instead of re-building workflows or forcing users into multiple inboxes, the ECC Connector provides a unified user experience, reduces disruption, and supports a smoother transition.

ECC Connector in action

<Coming Soon>

Curious how this can work in your landscape?

If you're in the midst of an S/4HANA transition and struggling with disconnected ECC workflows, the ECC Connector for Task Center could be your answer. Reach out to SAP or our partner MHP to join the program and bring clarity and cohesion to your task management.

entry.title: Bank Guarantee (Work Around)
entry.content:
Hi,

Our client has a requirement related to Bank Guarantees, which I found falls under the licensed scope 2NZ.

Requirement Overview:

  1. Initially, the client issues a Bank Guarantee to the customer. This should be reflected as a note to the accounts in the Financial Statement Version (FSV) because it is a contingent liability.

  2. If the customer invokes the Bank Guarantee, it should then be recognized under liabilities as "Bank Guarantee Payable."

  3. Subsequently, the client settles the payment against the Bank Guarantee.

Additional Notes:
1. Bank Guarantees may be issued in various forms, such as Performance Guarantees, Warranty Guarantees, etc.

Please provide work around process for BG

entry.title: Re: Report on percentage billing on each project in a month
entry.content:
@AnkushSingh Something like this

SELECT "User ID",
	"Project name",
	"Number of hours billed", (
	"Number of hours billed" / (SELECT SUM("Number of hours billed") FROM "AGGREGATION_TEST" AS T WHERE
	--T."Project name" = AT."Project name" AND 
	T."User ID" = AT."User ID")) * 100 AS "Percentage"
FROM "AGGREGATION_TEST" AT
ORDER BY "Project name" ASC

entry.title: How to Lead the Charge in Software Innovation: The Strategic Importance of Research and Development
entry.content:
For organizations aiming to lead in the digital age, R&D is not optional; it’s strategic. The ever-evolving tech landscape, the capability to innovate not only provides a competitive edge but is imperative for thriving. And our commitment to R&D empowers this innovation, driving growth and ensuring our relevance in the market. 

The Significance of R&D in Software Innovation 

R&D is the engine that drives our software development efforts. It involves experimenting with new ideas and technologies, even when the outcome is not known. This process is quite different from standard software development, which focuses on building specific products based on market demands or client needs. While software development focuses on delivering functional products within defined schedules, R&D is all about pushing the boundaries to find new solutions for challenging problems and understanding that the outcome might not always be foreseen. 

How R&D is Different from Software Development? 

To acknowledge the pivotal role of R&D, it's important to differ it from traditional software development: 

Objective - R&D emphasizes experimenting with ideas and technologies without instant productization, whereas software development focuses on developing specific products based on the requirements. 

Approach - R&D involves creating new solutions for societal problems with unexpected outcomes. On the other hand, software development aims to resolve specific business problems with clear objectives. 

Timeline - R&D processes can stretch across years without set timeframes, while software development operates within strict timelines to deliver functioning software. 

Understanding these Differences Underscores Why R&D is Vital for Sustained Innovation and Long-Term Success 

It highlights how R&D goes beyond immediate product goals to lay the groundwork for future breakthroughs, ensuring organizations remain adaptive and forward-looking.

Fostering a Culture of Innovation 

It is important to foster an environment that encourages innovation within software development teams. This involves encouraging cross-functional collaboration to break down silos, fostering continuous learning to stay current with technological advancements, and promoting an open mindset receptive to new ideas. Identifying and rewarding innovative efforts further motivates teams to push their limits. And taking time for exploration helps developers to brainstorm and experiment, leading to development of creative solutions. 

Tech-Driven Transformation 

Today, you can expect the global digital transformation market to value around USD 10,944.65 billion by 2032, driven by technological advancements. Hence, for navigating the ever-changing technology landscape you’ll need a well-rounded approach to adopting new tools and technologies. Optimizing current technology ensures we maximize value and stay current with changing business needs without unnecessary expenditure. Identifying futuristic technologies and experimenting with innovations helps us to maintain a competitive edge. This strategic approach ensures that our technology investments align with our business vision and drive innovation. 

The Role in Technology Transformation 

Involvement in transforming the IT function to align with strategic priorities is crucial. By asking pertinent questions about IT capabilities and ensuring they are closely aligned with business goals, significant improvements in financial performance can be achieved. Strengthening the technology function and integrating it with business operations is essential for successful digital transformation. 

Integrating AI in R&D 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) transforms R&D by speeding up innovation and product development. Huge conglomerates such as Amazon and Alphabet, are adapting to AI-based R&D for their future applications. AI-powered tools help improve market research, design, prototyping, simulations, and customer personalization, considerably optimizing processes and efficiency. Embracing AI within R&D encourages breakthrough innovations and comes with new dimensions of efficiency. Also, educating the workforce on AI is imperative to harness its full potential and maintain competitiveness. 

Conclusion 

Pioneering the lead in software innovation needs an unwavering dedication to R&D. By encouraging a culture that embraces AI, strategic technology adoption, and values experimentation, organizations are positioned at the forefront of the industry. Leadership in these areas is essential to driving innovation, achieving sustainable growth, and securing a place in the tech world ahead.

entry.title: Session 3 Recap & Highlights– Unpacking SAP Business Data Cloud for Enterprise Architects
entry.content:
I opened the session by highlighting the strategic intent of the webcast series. Having covered the high-level vision of data and AI in earlier episodes, this third instalment focused on unpacking SAP Business Data Cloud specifically for enterprise architects. We were joined by a strong lineup of speakers from CS&D, Product Management, and the Enterprise Architecture & Advisory teams — each bringing a unique perspective, from product strategy and architecture to real-world implementation and future roadmap.

@AmoghUmbarkar  opened the main content with a compelling message: data must move from being a burden to becoming a strategic enabler. He emphasized how BDC tackles key challenges in enterprise data landscapes — including fragmented integration efforts, siloed systems, and the growing need for faster, governed access to insights.

“Let’s not forget — data alone is not the answer. We need to embed it into the business process to guide all functions.”

He talked about concept of “Data as a Product,” moving away from traditional “Data as a Service” models. Amogh drew an analogy between the old ways of fetching water and today’s ready to consume bottled water to illustrate the shift toward curated, ready-to-consume, business-context-rich data products. These products empower users, enabling organizations to move from “gatekeepers to shopkeepers.”

Amogh also showcased how SAP itself acts as “Customer Zero,” demonstrating real use cases — including a 60-day implementation of a working capital intelligent app that achieved high business adoption and governance alignment.

@ralph_richter  provided a deep dive into the architecture of SAP’s Business Data Cloud. He explained how SAP leverages a layered, modular architecture built on:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud for visualization and planning
  • SAP Data Sphere for governance, federation, and lineage
  • Databricks integration for AI/ML and data science
  • Zero-copy data sharing to minimize replication
  • Business Data Cockpit for orchestration and lifecycle management

Ralph also introduced SAP’s growing library of intelligent applications, starting with Working Capital Insights, that address key business questions around payment terms, DSO, and capital optimization.

 The technically rich portion of the session came from Axel Meier, who demystified the “Data Product” concept in SAP’s vision. Axel described data products as semantically enriched, business-contextualized, and metadata-driven datasets, available in a zero-copy, fully governed environment. These are built for reuse, integrated by design, and updated continuously.

        “Data products are not just one-to-one copies of CDS views. They come pre-integrated, enriched, and detached from source systems to enable scalable, cross-application reuse.”

Axel Meier also highlighted SAP’s collaboration with Databricks, showcasing how SAP uses Delta Lake and Delta Sharing protocols for seamless external consumption. He demonstrated live how financial planning data products appear in both SAP and Databricks environments, underlining the ease and power of cross-platform access.

What’s Next and How to Get Started

Ralph Richter concluded the session with an invitation to explore BDC through:

  • BDC Discovery Workshops – Offered in 2-hour, half-day, and full-day formats.

  • Free Landscape Assessments – Evaluating readiness and tailoring BDC journeys.

  • Service Offerings – From MaxAttention to implementation accelerators.

Session 3 of SAP’s Business Data Cloud series was a masterclass in how to shift from legacy data integration to future-ready, intelligent data ecosystems. The emphasis on business-aligned data products, seamless integration with hyperscalers, and intelligent applications provides a powerful framework for architects to rethink enterprise data strategies.

Note: Slides from the session and the recording would be only available to the registered attendees. So if you have not registered, please register now using the link below: https://go4.events.sap.com/shift-eaa-bdc-webinar/en_us/home.html

Important links:

  1. Architecture Center: https://architecture.learning.sap.com/
  2. Enterprise Architect Learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-enterprise-architecture
  3. SAP Business Data Cloud Learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/courses/introducing-sap-business-data-cloud

@PaulKurchina @moshenaveh @ralph_richter @JaSoN_Luo @Timo  @Gaurish-Dessai 

SAP Business Data Cloud   SAP Datasphere   Data and Analytics   SAP Analytics Cloud   BW (SAP Business Warehouse)

entry.title: [Part 1] The Future of AI in SAP LeanIX for Enterprise Architects: SAPPHIRE ’25 unpacked
entry.content:
Starting with the Basics: What is an AI Agent and why are they important for Enterprise Architects?

Surely, you already heard terms like “Joule” or “CoPilot” already, and most likely, you have worked with agents already. Let’s get started with some basics – what actually is an AI Agent? Gartner provides the following definition:

"AI Agents are autonomous or semiautonomous software entities that use AI techniques to perceive, make decisions, take actions and achieve goals in their digital or physical environments."[1]

On top of this, Gartner also shares an outlook on the rising importance of agentic AI: While a back in 2024, just a few months ago, less than 1% of enterprise applications included agentic AI, a significant increase is to be expected: According to the research, 33% of all enterprise software applications will include agentic AI. 15% of all day-to-day work decisions will be made autonomously through agentic AI.[2]

For architects responsible for maintaining transparency among business, application, technology and data, these numbers highlight a critical need to evolve how architecture is captured, governed, and planned. SAP LeanIX, the Enterprise Architecture Management tool, will soon accommodate this need.

 

Launch of the AI Agent Hub in SAP LeanIX

So, how will SAP LeanIX accommodate this need? Let’s take a look at SAPPHIRE: One of the visions, that clearly stood out at this year’s event, was the AI Agent Hub – shared by André Christ and Philipp Herzig. The AI Agent Hub in SAP LeanIX will serve as a centralized cockpit, allowing users to manage and govern AI agents throughout their lifecycle. Certainly, this does not only focus on SAP AI Agents like Joule, but spans Standard SAP Agents, Custom AI Agents (built in Joule Studio), and Non-SAP Agents (e.g. Microsoft CoPilot).

Hint: if you want to get started with SAP LeanIX, you might want to read Jansi’s publication on how she has gained her first experiences with SAP LeanIX here.

Vision is, that SAP LeanIX detects AI Agents within the Architecture via a Discovery Inbox – similar but not to be mix up with the SAP Landscape Discovery provided in SAP LeanIX (probably very familiar to our experts of the SAP integrated Toolchain)

 

 

Potential: What are possible use cases for the AI Agent Hub in SAP LeanIX?

While the AI Agent Hub will be made available in SAP LeanIX, it will be closely connected to SAP Signavio and SuccessFactors, to broaden potential for process improvement and workforce strategy. In fact, process improvement and workforce strategy are two of many use cases for the AI Agent Hub in SAP LeanIX. Let’s take a closer look at some of the use cases:

  1. Transparency and Compliance:
    As companies adopt more AI agents across different business areas, it becomes critical to know exactly which agent is doing what, and what data it’s using. This is especially important for compliance with data privacy regulations like GDPR or industry-specific standards.
    The SAP LeanIX AI Agent Hub gives a clear, centralized view of all AI agents in use—showing which provider they come from, what business function they support, and what data they consume. This helps IT and compliance teams quickly spot risks, avoid data misuse, and ensure all AI activity stays within governance policies. On top of this, SAP LeanIX provides an AI Agent Radar to visualize and communicate agent approval level within an organisation, again increasing transparency.

  2. Exploration:
    The SAP LeanIX AI Agent Hub not only highlights AI agents in use and mapped to SAP Business Capabilities, but it also helps explore potential for AI Agents. When selecting an agent, users are presented with a detailed factsheet that includes key information such as, Value drivers the agent supports (e.g., efficiency gains, cost reduction), Applications the agent integrates with, and direct links to resources like SAP for Me, offering the possibility to activate the agent. The hub enables enterprise architects, IT planners, and business stakeholders to make informed decisions about where and how to introduce an AI agent across the organization.

  3. Assessing process efficiency:
    The SAP LeanIX AI Agent Hub, when integrated with SAP Signavio, will create an even more powerful synergy between enterprise architecture and business process intelligence. With SAP Signavio offering detailed process analysis, such as process efficiency or deviations from SAP recommendations, and SAP LeanIX providing a comprehensive view of the application landscape, the Agent Hub can identify areas where AI agents might automate tasks, reduce manual effort, or replace underutilized systems. For example, if SAP Signavio highlights a slow or costly step in an order-to-cash process, and SAP LeanIX shows that multiple outdated systems are involved, the Agent Hub can suggest automation to streamline the step.

  4. Innovation potential by application:
    A question customers often ask once moved to SAP S/4HANA is around the value potential from an application centric view. With SAP Agent Hub the available AI agents can be identified from an application perspective and with the help of a prioritization in alignment with business goals potential AI candidates can be identified and planned for implementation.

  5. Value Realization:
    Once AI agents are identified and introduced into the organization the next question is about the value realization of the expected outcomes. SAP LeanIX will provide a dedicated Architecture Executive dashboard with KPI driven insights into the aspects of governance, adoption and the return on investment. These insights can then be turned into impactful decisions such as scalable re-training of employees with more value-adding skills.

  1. Evaluating impact on employee skills:
    As initially mentioned, the SAP LeanIX AI Agent Hub is planned to be integrated with SuccessFactors, SAP’s cloud solution to manage Human Resources. The aim of this linkage is to even better align workforce strategy with human-machine collaboration. Or in other words: it provides insights into the skill-level of the workforce, particularly focusing on AI Knowledge. Possible insights, generated by both SuccessFactors and the Agent Hub, could highlight the need for upskilling on strategic topics.


Digging deeper: How can the AI Agent Hub potentially fit into the SAP LeanIX Meta Model?

In the role of an Enterprise Architect, continuously working on mapping and detailing application landscapes with various tools including SAP LeanIX, the question arises: How will the AI Agents be represented in SAP LeanIX?

The SAP LeanIX Meta Model v4 comprises 12 factsheet types. To describe an agent, the factsheet types Application, Data Object, Provider and IT Component will be highly relevant. However, this is not the end of the story: In order to embed the AI Agent into the existing architecture repository, most likely the factsheet types Business Capability and Business Context will play a central role.

Hint: Remember use case #5 about Value Realization? Look what's above the Relations Explorer - this is whatthe Estimated Business Value could look like

 

Example: Joule Studio Pricing Negotiator

Let’s pick “Joule Studio Pricing Negotiator” as an example to get a better understanding on what this could look like:

  • Joule Studio is part of SAP Build and allows organizations to create and deploy custom Joule agents across SAP and non-SAP systems. One of these AI agents is the so-called “Pricing Negotiator”: This agent can be designed to help procurement managers develop strategies for negotiating better pricing, warranty terms, delivery timelines, support services, and payment terms with suppliers. 
  • When aiming to describe the AI Agent as a first step, one can map it individually as an Application factsheet with the sub-type AI Agent, calling it “Joule Studio Pricing Negotiator”. Common Data Objects the AI Agent works with might include invoices, payment histories, contracts, and many others.
  • Since this example uses SAP technology, SAP would be listed as the Provider of the Joule Studio Pricing Negotiator. Finally, Joule itself can be documented as an IT component alongside others. However, just because it’s Joule, it does not necessarily mean it is SAP. Bear in mind the various partnerships and integrations, that rally around SAP Joule.

 

AI Agent Linkage with Business Architecture in SAP LeanIX

We have already examined the use cases for the AI Agent Hub. It is clear to us, that we can fulfill the above-mentioned use cases only, if the various AI agents are thoroughly integrated and linked to Business Architecture. This close alignment ensures that AI agents effectively support and enhance the overall enterprise architecture. In SAP LeanIX, the factsheet types Organization, Business Capability and Business Context represent the Business Architecture.

To achieve the linkage of Business Architecture with Application, Data and Technical Architecture, the AI Hub will extend possibilities for Business Capability Mapping, meaning that Business Capabilities in SAP LeanIX will be mapped to the respective AI Agents. The linkage between those worlds will be possible via the factsheet types Application and Business Capability.

Let’s double-click on the factsheet type Application: It describes software systems or programs that process or analyze business data. Applications support business tasks, processes, or aspects of an organization's business model. The application fact sheet is a core fact sheet of SAP LeanIX as it is interconnected with many different fact sheets, such as the Business Capability.

What is “Business Capability” again? One definition that summarizes the term very well was elaborated by Kotusev: According to him, a Business Capability Model is a structured representation of an organization's core abilities that enable it to achieve its objectives and deliver value.

The SAP Business Capability Model, which can for example be found in the SAP LeanIX Reference Catalog, follows a three level-hierarchy:

  • Level 1 – Business Domain: These are broad, high-level categories representing major areas of the business.
    Example (e.g. Joule Pricing Negotiator): Sourcing and Procurement
  • Level 2 – Business Areas: These are more specific subcategories within each domain, detailing particular functions.
    Example (e.g. Joule Pricing Negotiator): Procurement Contract Management
  • Level 3 – Business Capability: These are the most granular elements describing specific business functions.
    Example (e.g. Joule Pricing Negotiator): Supplier Negotiation Management

In this mock-up, we see a Level 2 Business Capability Mapping with Applications and respective AI Agents. With this view, users can easily spot, which Business Domains are already supported by AI Agents (check mark), or could be supported in the future (greyed out). Or in other words: such functionality will increase transparency and support use case #1 from above.

Hint: Did you know that there is a Reference Catalog in SAP LeanIX, containing a set of predefined Business Capabilities based on industries? Learn how to navigate the Reference Catalog with this Click-Through Demo.

Additionally, there is the vision to provide an embedded AI Agent Portal, allowing users to identify new AI Agents based on filter options, such as specific Business Capabilities. Such possibility will simplify exploration and support use case #2 from above.

In this blog post, René and I outlined a vision of what the AI Agent Hub in SAP LeanIX could look like and how it will shape the future of enterprise architecture. Bear in mind, that we (unfortunately) do not own a crystal ball. As with any forward-looking concept, reality may evolve differently. But the direction is clear: AI agents will become an integral part of IT landscapes -and SAP LeanIX is ready for it.

Thank you for your interest and curiosity in this important topic. We are just at the beginning of a much larger conversation. Stay tuned for the second blog post of this series, where we will explore AI-driven innovations for Enterprise Architects and how they’re being brought to life in the SAP LeanIX ecosystem.

Best, René and Julia

 

[1] Gartner®, Innovation Insights: AI Agents, Tom Coshow, Erick Brethenoux, Gary Olliffe, Pieter den Hamer, Leinar Ramos, Avivah Litan. April 3 2024

[2] Gartner®,TSP 2025 Trends: Agentic AI — The Evolution of Experience, Jim Hare, Tom Coshow, Mark McDonald, Radu Miclaus, Sid Nag, February 24 2025

entry.title: Enable OR Logic in Advanced Filters in SAP Sales Cloud v2
entry.content:
Currently, SAP Sales Cloud v2 only supports AND logic in advanced filters. This means that all defined conditions must be true simultaneously for a record to be included in the result set. However, in many practical scenarios, there is a need to retrieve records that meet at least one of multiple conditions – i.e., applying OR logic.

Example Use Case:

We want to build a customer filter that includes:

  • All customers for whom I am the account owner,
    OR

  • All customers with the specific customer number 123456.

The goal is to retrieve the union of both sets – i.e., all customers matching either condition, not just those who match both. Currently, this is not possible due to the limitation of AND-only logic, which returns only records that meet both criteria, effectively resulting in an empty or overly narrow result.

Important Business Scenario: Outlook Integration

A major use case for this capability is in the Outlook integration, where filters determine which customers and contacts are synchronized. Without OR logic, it's not possible to configure synchronization rules such as:

  • Synchronize all contacts of customers I'm responsible for or of a key account (e.g., 123456).

This limits the ability to define meaningful and efficient synchronization scopes, especially in environments where sales reps manage both portfolios and strategic accounts.

Requested Enhancement:

Please enable OR logic in filter configuration, allowing records to be included when any one of multiple conditions is true. This would allow users to define flexible and realistic filter criteria, based on real-world business needs.

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Public Cloud - Controlling - Maintain Plan Ver...
entry.content:
Hola @FranciscoCosta 

Thank you for your question regarding the implementation of multiple cost planning versions in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

As you correctly noted, the ability to maintain multiple plan versions (a standard feature in on‑premise S/4HANA) is not available today in Public Edition.

Current Status

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2502): Does not include this feature currently.

Roadmap Outlook

SAP has not officially confirmed whether multi-version cost planning will be added in future Public Edition releases. You can track enhancements through the SAP Roadmap Explorer under Finance > Controlling or monitor quarterly release notes for updates.

 

Recommendations

Requirement

Solution

Multiple cost plan versions needed

Continue your on-premise system, or consider upgrading to Private Cloud Edition

Workarounds in Public Edition

Use different fiscal years, cost center hierarchy, or external tools for multiple plan scenarios.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Creating a new Crystal Report and using two Parameter...
entry.content:
I think you only need one parameter for the “reference date”. Since the other date is 30 days before, you need to take the reference date - 30.
I don't know if SAP BI can send an empty date.

To control the value of the date parameter, you can work with a formula in "field explorer"

or directly in the record selection filter.

If SAP feeds the "empty" date with a distant date, you can do:

if {?p_currentdate}<date(1900,1,1) then
  {command.dat}>=currentdate-30 and {command.dat}<=currentdate
else 
  {command.dat}>={?p_currentdate}-30 and {command.dat}<={?p_currentdate}

If you create a formula field, you will must call it to filter records :

entry.title: "DFF Generation Error in SAP TM FIORI 2023"
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

After upgrading to SAP TM FIORI 2023, I'm facing an issue when posting a DFF freight cost.

The freight charge calculation schema includes a percentage-based condition using the scale FRT_%_VLR_MERC. During processing, the system ends up calculating a negative final amount, which triggers the following error:

  • Save Cancelled
    Enter reference-from line and reference-to line for charge line 920
    Line no. 920: Charge type FRT_%_VLR_MERC can't have a negative value."

It seems that the system is not validating or handling cases where the base value used in the percentage calculation results in a negative amount.

Has anyone run into a similar problem after the 2023 update?
#SAPTM2023 #FIORI2023

entry.title: Re: Time confirmation with two assign 2 activity types to...
entry.content:
Dear Aoime

In SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) within S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, when you assign two activity types (A and B) to the same Work Center in the Costing tab, the system can only propose one activity type per operation during time confirmation (e.g., via IW41 or Fiori app "Find Maintenance Orders and Operations").

Why this happens:

  • The order operation references one activity type only — the one maintained in the operation detail.
  • Even if the Work Center supports multiple activity types (maintained in the Work Center’s costing tab), only the activity type entered in the order operation is proposed during confirmation.
  • The standard SAP logic assumes one activity type per operation unless the scenario explicitly allows for splitting.

Workaround Options:

  1. Split the operation into two separate operations:

    • Assign Activity Type A to one operation and Activity Type B to another.
    • This allows time confirmation separately for each activity type.

  2. Use different confirmation profiles or BAdI implementation (advanced):

    • If you're using Fiori or GUI, custom enhancement (via BAdI WORKORDER_CONFIRM) might allow dual activity-type processing logic.
    • However, this is not standard and would require development and rigorous testing.

  3. Manual adjustment (not ideal):

    • Post time confirmation for Activity Type A via IW41.
    • Then post a second confirmation manually for Activity Type B using the same operation and Work Center, by temporarily modifying the activity type in the order before confirming (requires coordination and can affect audit trail).

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Handle persons leaving the organisation when provisio...
entry.content:
Took a while to get it to work, but with some help from CY from SAP support I managed to configure provisioning based on EntraID groups.

How it works

If userA is part of EntraID group qas_users, userA will be created in S/4

If userA is locked in EntraID, userA gets locked in S/4

If userA is removed from EntraID, userA gets locked in S/4

If usera gets removed from EntraID group qas_users, userA will be locked in S/4

 

Setup

Source EntraID reads users. Target IAS_QAS has condition to only select users from qas_users group.

"condition": "($.emails EMPTY false) && ($.userName EMPTY false) && (isValidEmail($.emails[0].value)) && ($.groups[*].displayName contains 'qas_users')"

 

In transformation the user is added to IAS group sapqas

{
"constant": [
{
"id": "<group ID in IAS for group sapqas>"
}
],
"targetVariable": "assignGroup"

Now all new users initially are put in the sapqas group.

sapqas is the condition to use during reading IAS for S/4 provisioning

Source system Local_IAS_to_QAS

ias.user.filter = groups.display eq "qas_users"

All qas users are read from the qas_users group

Target system QAS-RFC

"user": {
        "condition": "(($.emails EMPTY false) || isValidEmail($.emails[0].value) && ($.groups[?(@.display ==='SAPQAS' )]))",

I now only select the users that have been put in the conditional group sapqas. If I would use the qas_users group, they would be excluded from processing if removed from the group in EntraID. 

So, need to work with the sapqas group. 

 {
                "condition": "$.groups[?(@.display === 'qas_users' )] empty true",
                "constant": "L",
                "targetPath": "$.LOCK"
            },

This condition checks if the user is still allowed to work in S/4 based on assignement of the group qas_users. If not assigned the user will be locked in S/4. Once the user is assigned again to the group, user will be unlocked by the default transformation 

 {
                "condition": "'${operationTypeVariable}' == 'updateEntity'",
                "constant": "U",
                "targetPath": "$.LOCK"
            },

 

I have multiple S/4 systems to provision from CIS. I made a local IAS target per backendsystem, so one for QAS, one for PRD etc. This makes the condition much easier.

entry.title: Re: Reconciliation Balances on the fly translation to euro
entry.content:
Hello @Gabor5 

Thank you for your question and for sharing the screenshots.

In the Reconciliation Balances app, the "On-the-Fly Translation to Euro" option performs a real-time conversion of local and transaction currency balances into EUR using the current system exchange rate (typically the M rate). This is done dynamically at the time of report execution, not based on historical exchange rates from the original postings.

This helps provide a consistent and comparable view of intercompany balances across different company codes  especially helpful when your group currency is EUR, as in your example with a German (EUR) and Romanian (RON) entity.

Why the Balances Appear Correct Only in EUR View:

  • The dynamic EUR conversion eliminates FX mismatches caused by different exchange rates applied in the original sender and receiver postings.
  • Views using transaction currency or global currency reflect original document values, which may differ due to:
  • Different exchange rate types or dates used at posting time
  • Rounding differences
  • Local valuation rules

Therefore, discrepancies in these currencies are expected and don’t indicate errors — they’re natural outcomes of decentralised posting logic. The EUR view is purely analytical and intended to simplify reconciliation, especially for group reporting.

While the term on-the-fly may not appear explicitly in SAP documentation, this behaviour is fully supported in the logic behind scope item 2CX (Intercompany Matching and Reconciliation) and reflected in SAP Help Portal guidance.

Let me know if you need further assistance

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: App Material Documents Overview - Ho can I indicate c...
entry.content:
Dear Dirk

Standard Limitation

  • Currently, the Manage Material Documents app (or similar apps like “Display Material Document”) does not include a column or icon that shows whether an attachment is present.
  • The attachment (via GOS or Document Management System/DMS) is only visible once you open the material document details.

Why This Happens

  • In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, customization options are limited compared to the On-Premise or Private Edition.
  • GOS (Generic Object Services) functionality is restricted in the cloud version.
  • Standard apps are delivered by SAP and cannot be freely enhanced with custom columns showing attachment status.

Here are the possible Workarounds

A. Use Document Info Records (DIR) and DMS

  • Instead of GOS attachments, manage attachments via Document Management System (DMS) and link them to material documents using Document Info Records.
  • Then use "Manage Documents" Fiori app to track and filter by document usage.

B. Custom CDS View + Custom App

  • Create a custom CDS view that reads from:
    • MKPF and MSEG (for material documents),
    • and links with SRGBTBREL or similar services tables (if accessible in public edition),
  • This CDS view could include an indicator column showing whether an attachment exists.
  • Then expose this CDS view via a Custom Analytical App or List Report.

C. SAP BTP Extension (Side-by-Side App)

  • Build a small UI5 app or dashboard on SAP BTP that uses public APIs (if available) to retrieve material documents and show an “Attachment Exists” flag.
  • This app can then be added to the launchpad via an In-App extension.

D. Raise SAP Influence Request

  • Since this is a recurring need for logistics teams, consider raising a Customer Influence request or check if one already exists on the SAP Customer Influence portal.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh IS Subscribe Access Denied
entry.content:
Yes, there were new access rules introduced in a recent update of EMIS. I ended up creating a new event mesh instance with this config:

{
"namespace": "sap/em/integration",
"resources": {
"units": 10
},
"rules": {
"queueRules": {
"publishFilter": [
"*"
],
"subscribeFilter": [
"*"
]
},
"topicRules": {
"publishFilter": [
"*"
],
"subscribeFilter": [
"*"
]
}
}
}
The wildcard lets you publish and subscribe to any topic/rule of your instance.

entry.title: Re: Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories & Analytic Applications
entry.content:
Hi @Moe_Kai,

With the 2025 Q3 QRC, which is the next phase of our Deprecation Plan, there will be a visual indicator to distinguish between a Classic Story and Optimized Experience within the File Repository, Recent Files, and so on. Within the File Repository we have also separated out the Filter Options to make it easier to find those respective assets. With the System Overview Dashboard that is provided to administrators, under Feature Adoption we provide statistics what percentage of objects that are opened are a Classic Story, Classic Application, or Optimized Experience. There are additional visualizations that identify the location of these objects.

Regards,

Tunir

entry.title: Re: How to get details of Users and their roles in ICM
entry.content:
@kr_pavankumar 

Below SQL can provide some hints to your Question and tweak according to your end... 

Kindly accept the solution and hit Like Button.

SELECT u.userid, 'Assigned Role' AS Source, d.name AS Domain, r.name AS Role
FROM csi_user u
INNER JOIN CSI_PRINCIPALROLE pr ON pr.PRINCIPALSEQ=u.userseq AND pr.principaltype=1
INNER JOIN CSI_role r ON pr.roleseq=r.roleseq
INNER JOIN csi_domain d ON r.domaintype=d.domaintype
UNION
SELECT u.userid, 'Inherited from Group', d.name, r.name
FROM CSI_User u
INNER JOIN CSI_USERGROUP ug ON u.userseq=ug.USERSEQ
INNER JOIN csi_group g ON g.groupseq=ug.groupseq
INNER JOIN CSI_PRINCIPALROLE pr ON pr.principalseq=g.groupseq AND pr.principaltype=2
INNER JOIN CSI_role r ON pr.roleseq=r.roleseq
INNER JOIN csi_domain d ON r.domaintype=d.domaintype
UNION
SELECT u.userid, 'Global', g.domain_name, g.role_name
FROM csi_user u, (SELECT d.name AS domain_name, r.name AS role_name FROM csi_role r INNER JOIN csi_domain d ON r.domaintype=d.domaintype WHERE r.isglobal=1) g
UNION
SELECT u.userid, 'Inherited from Title', d.name, r.name FROM csi_user u
INNER JOIN 
(SELECT
    upper(t0.userId) AS userId,
	t0.titleseq
FROM
	(
		SELECT DISTINCT pos.ruleElementOwnerSeq AS positionSeq,
		pos.name,
		pos.managerSeq,
		pos.titleSeq,
		**bleep**.name AS titleName,
		pos.payeeSeq,
		part.firstName,
		part.lastName,
		part.userId,
		payee.payeeId,
		(CASE
			WHEN pos.payeeSeq IS NULL
			OR (pos.payeeSeq IS NOT NULL
			AND pos.effectiveStartDate > part.effectiveStartDate) THEN pos.effectiveStartDate
			ELSE part.effectiveStartDate
		END) AS effectiveStartDate,
		(CASE
			WHEN pos.payeeSeq IS NULL
			OR (pos.payeeSeq IS NOT NULL
			AND pos.effectiveEndDate < part.effectiveEndDate ) THEN pos.effectiveEndDate
			ELSE part.effectiveEndDate
		END) AS effectiveEndDate,
		(CASE
			WHEN pos.payeeSeq IS NULL
			OR (pos.payeeSeq IS NOT NULL
			AND pos.removeDate < part.removeDate ) THEN pos.removeDate
			ELSE part.removeDate
		END) AS removeDate,
		(CASE
			WHEN pos.payeeSeq IS NULL
			OR (pos.payeeSeq IS NOT NULL
			AND pos.createDate > part.createDate ) THEN pos.createDate
			ELSE part.createDate
		END) AS createDate
	FROM
		CS_Position pos
	INNER JOIN CS_Title **bleep** ON
		pos.titleSeq = **bleep**.ruleElementOwnerSeq
	LEFT OUTER JOIN CS_Participant part ON
		pos.payeeSeq = part.payeeSeq
		AND pos.effectiveStartDate < part.effectiveEndDate
		AND pos.effectiveEndDate > part.effectiveStartDate
		AND pos.createDate < part.removeDate
		AND pos.removeDate > part.createDate
	LEFT OUTER JOIN CS_Payee payee ON
		part.payeeSeq = payee.payeeSeq
		AND payee.effectiveStartDate = part.effectiveStartDate
		AND payee.removeDate = part.removeDate
	WHERE
	    **bleep**.islast=1 and
		**bleep**.removeDate = (
			SELECT MAX(removeDate)
		FROM
			CS_Period)) t0	
WHERE
	( (t0.REMOVEDATE = date'2200-01-01')
	AND (UPPER(t0.USERID) = 'XXXXXXXX'))) usertitle ON upper(u.userid) = usertitle.userid
INNER JOIN CSI_PRINCIPALROLE pr ON pr.principalseq=usertitle.titleseq AND pr.principaltype=3
INNER JOIN CSI_role r ON pr.roleseq=r.roleseq
INNER JOIN csi_domain d ON r.domaintype=d.domaintype
ORDER BY userid, SOURCE, DOMAIN, role

entry.title: Re: Error with Add Member functionality in SAC
entry.content:
Hi @pragnya_m , 

Using "Add Member", you can book new values on the unbooked member combinations. You know which unbooked members you are going to add. 

When you trigger "Add Member" from a table cell, the members of the outer dimensions (those to the left of the trigger cell) are inherited from that row. The new member added for the trigger dimension will be unbooked.

In your example, you are going to book a new value for BU "Products".  So you can trigger "Add Member" from "BU" dimension in the row "Products" (the cell circled in red).  A new line will be added below, where the member of Costcenter ("CC1") will be inherited from the same row.  You can then choose "Products"(which should be unbooked) for "BU" dimension,  and choose members for the remaining dimensions. 

Sarau

entry.title: Re: Can we do value mapping through MDG Consolidation ?
entry.content:
Hi Saravanan,

Thanks again. If I have understood your reply correctly, kindly confirm my inline comments below:

1. This particular adapter will utilise the value mapping already maintained in the system for the mapping. If value mapping is maintained between source system and consolidating system, the adapter will map the values else the inbound value will be considered. - so does it mean that, the custom code implemented in the Adapter class does not write any value mapping entries into MDG, it only reads the value mapping maintained in MDG? I am just trying to understand this part correctly. Also kindly clarify what you meant by "else the inbound value will be considered". what inbound value are you refering to?

2. Both options has its advantage and value addition.
If using ETL tool like BODS, it will be simpler in sense of value mapping where you can review the data pre and post conversion of values. But the value mapping has to be maintained in BODS for ETL purpose and same values to be maintained in MDG for MDG replication. -  are you saying, apart from maintaining the value mapping entries in BODS, we also have to maintain the same manually in MDG? or we can just upload the value mapping file into MDG that can be generated from BODS ater the data is laoded?
If using MDG consolidation for value mapping, all changes need to be visualized in the consolidation FIORI App. Performance and sizing need to be considered for better processing. But value mapping is maintained only in MDG system. - here we have around 200K vendors that are being loaded into MDG from 3 source systems. But the value mapping(conversions) would be hardly for 10-15 attributes in vendor master. Do you still see any issue here if we do through adapter class for this small number of mapping entries?   

Looking forward to your response.

Regards, T

entry.title: How to add a new input / selection to an existing Dialog box that has already been extended?
entry.content:
Hello,

I have been tasked with extending a Fiori app with an adaptation project. The app I am looking into is "Find Maintenance Orders and Operation" (F2173).

I am trying to add a new input / selection to a dialog box that opens when a user presses "Change Assignment". This dialog is part of an extension that was created by SAP. I want to be able to add another field to the fragment using a new extension but nothing new seems to display in the dialogue box. I would also need to extend the project with a controller but the override methods that are included in the controller extension (onInit, onBeforeRendering, onAfterRendering, etc.) do not get triggered during the press of the button. The method that handles the button within the SAP logic is called "onChangeAssignmentPressed" which is also part of a controller extension by SAP. Ideally we would want to override that method but is that even possible? If not, How else can we go about this by getting the additional section in our fragment extension to work with a possible new method that would somehow reach the logic that handles the existing "Change Assignment" button? 

When the user presses the "Change Assignment" button, a dialog box is displayed with selections in which we would like to another one in this window for "Division".

 

I have noticed that the app utilizes Function Imports which we are not really familiar with but it seems like we would need to add division here as well but these are not allowed to be changed. How do we proceed without being able to make a change here (not sure if needed)?

 

The existing XML logic for the SAP extension regarding the change assignment dialog box is this:

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns:smartform="sap.ui.comp.smartform"
  xmlns:smartfield="sap.ui.comp.smartfield" xmlns:layout="sap.ui.layout" id="ChangeAssignmentFragment"
  xmlns:customData="http://schemas.sap.com/sapui5/extension/sap.ui.core.CustomData/1">
  <Dialog title="{@i18n>xbut.changeAssignment}" contentWidth="20em" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDialog" afterOpen="afterDialogOpened" afterClose="afterDialogClosed">
    <content>
      <smartform:SmartForm flexEnabled="false" editTogglable="false" editable="true" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentSmartForm">
        <smartform:layout>
          <smartform:Layout id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentLayout" columnsL="1" columnsM="1" emptySpanL="1" emptySpanM="0" labelSpanL="0" labelSpanM="0"></smartform:Layout>
        </smartform:layout>
        <smartform:groups>
          <smartform:Group id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentGroup">
            <smartform:GroupElement objectBindings="{viewModel>/Operationworkcenter}" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmenWorkCenterGroupElement">
              <smartform:label>
                <Label text="{/#ChangeAssignmentInputType/Operationworkcenter/@sap:label}" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmenWorkCenterLabel"/>
              </smartform:label>
              <ComboBox visible="{= ${viewModel>/isMassEdit} && !${viewModel>isSingleMode} }"
                id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentWorkCenterMassEditComboBox" change="onMassEditComboBoxChanged" selectedKey="{viewModel>selectedKey}"
                items="{viewModel>massEditValues}" customData:smartFieldId="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentWorkCenterField">
                <items>
                  <core:Item key="{viewModel>key}" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentWorkCenterMassEditItem" text="{viewModel>value}"/>
                </items>
              </ComboBox>
              <smartfield:SmartField visible="{= !${viewModel>/isMassEdit} || ${viewModel>isSingleMode} }"
                id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentWorkCenterField" value="{Operationworkcenter}"/>
            </smartform:GroupElement>
            <smartform:GroupElement id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPlantGroupElement" objectBindings="{viewModel>/Plant}">
              <smartform:label>
                <Label text="{/#ChangeAssignmentInputType/Plant/@sap:label}" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPlantLabel"/>
              </smartform:label>
              <ComboBox visible="{= ${viewModel>/isMassEdit} && !${viewModel>isSingleMode} }"
                id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPlantMassEditComboBox" change="onMassEditComboBoxChanged" selectedKey="{viewModel>selectedKey}"
                items="{viewModel>massEditValues}" customData:smartFieldId="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPlantField">
                <items>
                  <core:Item key="{viewModel>key}" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPlantMassEditItem" text="{viewModel>value}"/>
                </items>
              </ComboBox>
              <smartfield:SmartField visible="{= !${viewModel>/isMassEdit} || ${viewModel>isSingleMode} }"
                id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPlantField" value="{Plant}"/>
            </smartform:GroupElement>
            <smartform:GroupElement id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentResponsibleGroupElement" objectBindings="{viewModel>/Operationpersonresponsible}">
              <smartform:label>
                <Label id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentResponsibleLabel" text="{/#ChangeAssignmentInputType/Operationpersonresponsible/@sap:label}"/>
              </smartform:label>
              <ComboBox visible="{= ${viewModel>/isMassEdit} && !${viewModel>isSingleMode} }"
                id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPersonResponsibleMassEditComboBox" change="onMassEditComboBoxChanged"
                selectedKey="{viewModel>selectedKey}" items="{viewModel>massEditValues}"
                customData:smartFieldId="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPersonResponsibleField">
                <items>
                  <core:Item key="{viewModel>key}" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPersonResponsibleMassEditItem" text="{viewModel>value}"/>
                </items>
              </ComboBox>
              <smartfield:SmartField visible="{= !${viewModel>/isMassEdit} || ${viewModel>isSingleMode} }"
                id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentPersonResponsibleField" value="{Operationpersonresponsible}"/>
            </smartform:GroupElement>
          </smartform:Group>
        </smartform:groups>
      </smartform:SmartForm>
    </content>
    <beginButton>
      <Button id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentOkButton" text="{@i18n>xbut.changeAssignment}" press="onOkPressed" type="Emphasized" />
    </beginButton>
    <endButton>
      <Button id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentCancelButton" text="{@i18n>xbut.cancel}" press="onCancelPressed"/>
    </endButton>
  </Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

Within my new fragment I attempted to add:

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns:smartform="sap.ui.comp.smartform"
    xmlns:smartfield="sap.ui.comp.smartfield" xmlns:layout="sap.ui.layout" id="ChangeAssignmentFragment"
    xmlns:customData="http://schemas.sap.com/sapui5/extension/sap.ui.core.CustomData/1">
    <Dialog title="{@i18n>xbut.changeAssignment}" contentWidth="20em" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDialog" afterOpen="afterDialogOpened" afterClose="afterDialogClosed">
        <content>
            <smartform:SmartForm flexEnabled="false" editTogglable="false" editable="true" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentSmartForm">
                <smartform:layout>
                    <smartform:Layout id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentLayout" columnsL="1" columnsM="1" emptySpanL="1" emptySpanM="0" labelSpanL="0" labelSpanM="0"></smartform:Layout>
                </smartform:layout>
                <smartform:groups>
                    <smartform:Group id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentGroup">
                        <smartform:GroupElement id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDivisionGroupElement" objectBindings="{viewModel>/zzdivision}">
                            <smartform:label>
                                <Label id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDivisionLabel" text="Division"/>
                                <!-- <Label id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDivisionLabel" text="{/#ChangeAssignmentInputType/zzdivision/@sap:label}"/> -->
                            </smartform:label>
                            <ComboBox visible="{= ${viewModel>/isMassEdit} && !${viewModel>isSingleMode} }"
                                id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDivisionMassEditComboBox" change="onMassEditComboBoxChanged"
                                selectedKey="{viewModel>selectedKey}" items="{viewModel>massEditValues}"
                                customData:smartFieldId="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDivisionField">
                                <items>
                                    <core:Item key="{viewModel>key}" id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDivisionMassEditItem" text="{viewModel>value}"/>
                                </items>
                            </ComboBox>
                            <smartfield:SmartField visible="{= !${viewModel>/isMassEdit} || ${viewModel>isSingleMode} }"
                                id="orderAndOperationChangeAssignmentDivisionField" value="{zzdivision}"/>
                        </smartform:GroupElement>
                    </smartform:Group>
                </smartform:groups>
            </smartform:SmartForm>
        </content>
    </Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

I would appreciate any feedback that would help us keep moving forward. Please let me know if any additional details are needed. 

Thank you,

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Changing User Status With STATUS_CHANGE_EXTERN
entry.content:
Since your error message is 'user status is not allowed', I would assume something is wrong with your status profile ZPM_EQI (and not the corresponding business transactions - these are used to control the system status).

I would do the following:

  • Find out the current user status of your object, e.g. using report CRM_ORDER_READ
  • Check whether the status order number of your status EAPRO is within the range that is defined for your current object status.

entry.title: Re: How to restore task standard TS00008267 SAP Workflow
entry.content: Change it manually or if you prefer do a Transport of Copy from another system to revert the changes completely. Don't forget to remove the workflow task from your transport request, no need to transport it to another system.
